archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

Exports BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES constant array and buildArchetypeMap helper — pure type/constant definitions with no business logic orchestration.
Pure functions (isBreakingChange, inferSignificance, classifyItem) for categorizing PRD items — stateless helpers with no I/O.
Async collectBranchWork() reads git history and .rex/prd.json to identify completed branch items — data fetching and business logic orchestration.
filterItemsByBranchScope() and detectBranchLifecycle() apply timestamp-based filtering logic to work items — domain logic orchestration.
saveBranchWorkRecord/loadBranchWorkRecord handle JSON file I/O for persistent branch work records — state persistence with high fan-in expected.
generateCallGraphFindings() analyzes call graph edges to emit architectural findings — domain analysis with business logic.
analyzeCallGraph() uses TypeScript AST parsing to extract function call graphs with incremental support — complex analysis orchestration.
analyzeClassifications() and enrichClassificationsWithLLM() orchestrate file archetype classification with LLM enrichment fallback — multi-step business logic.
callClaude() wraps vendor-neutral LLM API calls — API client abstraction layer.
readCompletionStatus() reads .rex/prd.json and extracts completion state, stats, and inconsistencies — data fetching and domain logic.
analyzeComponents() extracts React component definitions and JSX usages via AST — complex analysis orchestration.
generateContext() builds CONTEXT.md output from analysis results — output generation orchestration.
createSnapshot/computeDeltas/loadLatestReport track zone metric changes across runs — metrics computation and persistence.
enrichBatch() orchestrates batch LLM enrichment with retry logic and response aggregation — multi-step API orchestration.
Exports pass configs, prompt builders, and batch configuration helpers — configuration and strategy definitions for the enrichment pipeline.
tryParseJSON, extractFindings, mergeZonesByName, deduplicateFindings are pure parsing/transformation helpers — stateless utility functions.
enrichZonesPerZone() orchestrates per-zone incremental enrichment with concurrent processing — complex async orchestration.
enrichZonesWithAI() is the main AI enrichment orchestrator with batching and content-hash skipping — top-level pipeline orchestration.
analyzeImports() uses TypeScript AST to extract import graphs with module resolution and incremental support — complex analysis service.
analyzeInventory() walks the file system with language/role classification, gitignore support, and caching — data collection orchestration.
generateLlmsTxt() builds structured Markdown output for LLM consumption — output generation service.
louvainPhase1/mergeSmallCommunities/splitLargeCommunities implement community detection algorithm for zone detection — algorithmic analysis service.
readManifest/writeManifest/updateManifestModule manage persistent analysis manifest JSON — state persistence store.
Pure analysis/detection logic — computes move-file findings from zone/import data with no side effects, state, or I/O. Used as a shared utility by the analysis pipeline.
deriveNextSteps() derives prioritized actionable work items from architectural findings — domain logic orchestration.
assessAllZoneRisks/computeRiskScore/classifyRiskLevel perform zone health scoring — domain analysis and classification logic.
detectServerRoutes() detects HTTP route handlers via comment and framework pattern analysis — domain analysis service.
emptyAnalyzeTokenUsage/accumulateTokenUsage/formatTokenUsage are pure token accounting helpers — stateless utility functions.
aggregateInventory/aggregateZones/writeWorkspaceOutput orchestrate multi-repo workspace aggregation — complex orchestration service.
buildPackageMap/computeCrossRepoCrossings resolve external imports to sibling workspace members — domain analysis and resolution logic.
Analyzer orchestration logic for workspace-level analysis
Hashing utility for zone change detection
Core zone detection and analysis service (Louvain)
